topic: Activepieces vs n8n 2026: Open-Source Automation Compared

key_points: Detailed setup and deployment comparison for self-hosters
Covers licensing differences (MIT vs Sustainable Use License) and their implications
Compares integration ecosystems: n8n 400+ vs Activepieces 150+
Discusses AI and MCP support advantage for Activepieces
Infrastructure cost comparison: both run on $10-20/month VPS
Honest self-hoster perspective on both tools
Activepieces setup takes 15-30 min vs n8n 30-60 min
Team productivity: onboarding in 2 hours (Activepieces) vs ~2 days (n8n)
Performance: Activepieces faster execution due to linear step-based architecture
n8n can feel sluggish in larger complex node trees
Use-case-driven comparison: n8n for depth/scale, Activepieces for simplicity
UI comparison: n8n canvas-based node graph vs Activepieces linear drag-and-drop
Activepieces feels closer to Zapier in ease-of-use
n8n stronger for complex technical workflows requiring deep node-level control
Both platforms free to self-host; cloud plans differ significantly in model
Open-source credentials: Activepieces fully MIT, n8n fair-code Sustainable Use License
n8n restricts SaaS embedding and competing products commercially
Activepieces allows unrestricted embedding in SaaS products
Both support Docker and Docker Compose deployment
n8n has larger contributor base (800+ developers)
Activepieces own positioning: same power as n8n with better UX
Native AI integrations: OpenAI and Claude built in as native blocks no plugins needed
280+ MCP servers supported natively in Activepieces
Activepieces cloud offers unlimited runs even on free/lower tiers
Targets teams wanting polished UI and faster onboarding over raw technical depth

stats.fact: n8n has 400+ integrations and a contributor base of 800+ developers
Activepieces has nearly 20,000 GitHub stars in 2026, up from 10,000 in November 2024
Activepieces has 100,000+ active installations and is Y Combinator backed
60% of Activepieces integrations (pieces) are contributed by the community
Activepieces supports 280+ MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for AI agent workflows
n8n cloud pricing: 24/month Starter (2,500 executions), 60/month Pro (10,000 executions), 800/month Business (40,000 executions)
Activepieces cloud pricing: free for 10 active flows with unlimited runs; $5/active flow/month above that; $25/month Plus plan
Self-hosting either platform requires a VPS with 2 vCPUs and 4 GB RAM at $10-20/month
Activepieces team onboarding takes ~2 hours vs ~2 days for n8n
Activepieces Docker Compose setup takes 15-30 minutes vs n8n 30-60 minutes
n8n was founded in 2019; Activepieces launched in 2022-2023
Activepieces 0.82.0 (April 2026) added AWS Bedrock support, egress proxy, real-time flow locking, and MCP tool to build flows via LLM

angles: MIT vs Sustainable Use License: why licensing matters more than ever for SaaS builders embedding automation
AI-first showdown: Activepieces 280+ native MCP server support vs n8n earlier-stage MCP integration
Pricing model disruption: flow-based unlimited runs (Activepieces) vs execution-based metered billing (n8n)
Onboarding speed: 2 hours vs 2 days — when ease-of-use beats raw technical power
The challenger vs incumbent dynamic: n8n 2019 head-start and 400+ integrations vs Activepieces faster growth and modern architecture
Self-hosting reality check: actual setup time, infrastructure costs, and maintenance burden for both tools in 2026
Enterprise vs startup fit: n8n HITL gates and sandboxing for safe AI execution vs Activepieces broad MCP surface for rapid AI agent building
outline: H2: Activepieces vs n8n at a Glance (2026 Snapshot)
H2: Licensing Showdown — MIT vs Sustainable Use License
H2: User Interface and Learning Curve
H2: AI Capabilities and MCP Support
H2: Pricing Comparison — Cloud and Self-Hosted
H2: When to Choose n8n vs Activepieces
